system: You are a technical support bot. Help the user configure their environment based on the docs.

messages.content: How do I configure Single Sign-On (SSO)?

chunks.content:
Single Sign-On (SSO) is an authentication scheme that allows a user to log in with a single ID and password to any of several related, yet independent, software systems. True SSO allows the user to log in once and access services without re-entering authentication factors. It improves security by reducing password fatigue and centralizing access control. 

To configure Okta SSO:
1. Log into your Okta admin portal.
2. Navigate to Applications -> Add Application.
3. Create a new SAML 2.0 app integration.
4. Copy your IdP Metadata URL into the AcmeCorp settings.

Single Sign-On (SSO) is an authentication scheme that allows a user to log in with a single ID and password to any of several related, yet independent, software systems. True SSO allows the user to log in once and access services without re-entering authentication factors. It improves security by reducing password fatigue and centralizing access control. 

To configure Azure AD SSO:
1. Go to the Azure Active Directory portal.
2. Select Enterprise Applications -> New application.
3. Choose Non-gallery application and name it.
4. Set up Single Sign-On using SAML and download the Federation Metadata XML.

Single Sign-On (SSO) is an authentication scheme that allows a user to log in with a single ID and password to any of several related, yet independent, software systems. True SSO allows the user to log in once and access services without re-entering authentication factors. It improves security by reducing password fatigue and centralizing access control. 

To configure Google Workspace SSO:
1. Open the Google Admin console.
2. Go to Web and mobile apps.
3. Click Add App -> Add custom SAML app.
4. Download the IdP metadata and upload it to the AcmeCorp security panel.
